type: CLAIM

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: I will own outlines/types/__init__.py and implement both the ssn type and your hash_sha256 type. Please send a DEFER+SPEC for your hash_sha256 requirement so I can implement it exactly as you need.

type: DEFER
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
spec: Add a new module-level type definition named hash_sha256 in outlines/types/__init__.py, placed after the existing isbn definition. Definition: hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'). This validates SHA-256 hash strings: exactly 64 hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f, A-F), case-insensitive. It must be a plain Regex instance (from outlines.types.dsl import Regex, already imported in the file) so it inherits Term.validate(), __get_pydantic_core_schema__, __get_pydantic_json_schema__, and DSL operations (optional, at_least, etc.) automatically. No other logic needed - just the one-line definition with the regex pattern '[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'.
note: hash_sha256 type per feature spec; thanks for owning the file

type: DEFER
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
spec: Add after the isbn definition (around line 72): a Regex-based type named 'ssn' that validates US Social Security Numbers in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format (3 digits, hyphen, 2 digits, hyphen, 4 digits), e.g.: ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}"). Include a short comment above it noting it matches the standard SSN format (digits only, XXX-XX-XXXX), similar in style to the isbn comment above it. No other changes needed (no need to add check-digit/area-number validation logic, just the format-matching Regex).
note: ssn type
